frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Honestly, I never understand why some women put on so much makeup and then call it confidence. Natural look is always better, otherwise it just feels like you're hiding behind layers. But at the same time, if someone completely stops taking care of herself, that also says a lot.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places women in a double bind: condemning visible makeup as deceptive while also judging women who do not maintain appearance. It encodes the 'natural beauty' preference, grooming policing, and the idea that a woman's appearance reflects her worth or character.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 36. Single. A divorced man living in Delhi. You are commenting under your real name on a Facebook post about a celebrity couple's divorce where the wife is seeking 50 percent of her husband's assets despite having worked for only two years of their eight year marriage. You are commenting on whether what she is asking for is justified and what it says about why women get married.
